nits.issue: buildSmsBody clamps with `.slice(0, SMS_MAX)` as a defensive tail-trim. If linkLen > SMS_MAX (pathological — e.g., a custom appBaseUrl > 19 chars longer than default), the final slice would cut inside the link and break clickability. Realistic link (~121 chars) is well under 140.

Fire-and-forget `void loadApprovalChannel(...).then(notifyApproval).catch(...)` is sound in common case (worker stays alive ≤4h waiting on LISTEN). Narrow race: if approval decided within ms of request and the Sendblue HTTP roundtrip is still in flight when the worker process tears down, the SMS may not send. Advisory-only race window.
buildSmsBody truncates the leading prefix slightly more aggressively than strictly required.

defaultSendblueSend duplicates SENDBLUE_API_URL constant + auth-header parsing logic from worker/src/tools/send_sms.ts. Two implementations could drift on error parsing (e.g., 200-with-error_message handling). Consider a shared raw-HTTP helper.

loadApprovalChannel swallows db errors silently (only console.error). If workspaces table is unreachable, user gets no SMS and only signal is stderr on worker container. Could emit approval_notify_failed event in this path too.
RAW_TOKEN constant contains uppercase 'TOKEN' which is base64url-legal but not actually base64url-shaped in spirit. Cosmetic; 43-char test below covers the realistic case.
